matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-user-006 title: user views the menu module: user source pages: 1,6,7 preconditions: user is logged in. test data: - no specific data required. test steps: 1. user clicks on the menu option. 2. server sends the menu details to the user. expected result: user sees the menu details.

test case id: tc-user-007 title: user places an order module: user source pages: 1,6,7 preconditions: user is logged in and the menu is available. test data: - item: pizza - quantity: 2 test steps: 1. user selects "pizza" from the menu. 2. user enters "2" as the quantity. 3. user clicks on the order button. 4. server processes the order. 5. server sends an order confirmation to the user. expected result: user sees an order confirmation message.

test case id: tc-user-001 title: user logs in successfully module: user source pages: 6,7,8 preconditions: user has a valid username and password. test data: - username: testuser - password: testpassword test steps: 1. user enters "testuser" as the username. 2. user enters "testpassword" as the password. 3. user clicks on the login button. 4. server handles the login request. 5. server verifies the credentials. 6. server returns a success message to the user. expected result: user is logged in successfully and sees a success message.

test case id: tc-restaurant-001 title: restaurant views the menu module: restaurant source pages: 1,6,7 preconditions: restaurant is logged in. test data: - no specific data required. test steps: 1. restaurant clicks on the menu option. 2. server sends the menu details to the restaurant. expected result: restaurant sees the menu details.

test case id: tc-user-008 title: user places an order with invalid item module: user source pages: 1,6,7 preconditions: user is logged in and the menu is available. test data: - item: nonexistentitem - quantity: 2 test steps: 1. user selects "nonexistentitem" from the menu. 2. user enters "2" as the quantity. 3. user clicks on the order button. 4. server processes the order. 5. server returns an error message to the user. expected result: user sees an error message indicating that the item does not exist.

test case id: tc-user-005 title: user attempts to log in without entering a password module: user source pages: 6,7,8 preconditions: user has a valid username. test data: - username: testuser - password: (empty) test steps: 1. user enters "testuser" as the username. 2. user leaves the password field empty. 3. user clicks on the login button. 4. server handles the login request. 5. server returns an error message to the user. expected result: user sees an error message indicating that the password cannot be empty.
